title: Transactions
content:
### SQL Transactions

A transaction is a sequence of SQL operations treated as a single logical unit of work. Transactions ensure data integrity through ACID properties.

---

### ACID Properties

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Atomicity | All operations succeed or all are rolled back |
| Consistency | Transaction brings database from one valid state to another |
| Isolation | Concurrent transactions do not interfere with each other |
| Durability | Committed transactions persist even after system failure |

### Transaction Control Statements

| Statement | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| BEGIN TRANSACTION | Starts a new transaction |
| COMMIT | Saves all changes made in the transaction |
| ROLLBACK | Undoes all changes made in the transaction |
| SAVEPOINT | Sets a savepoint within the current transaction |

### Basic Transaction Example

```sql
BEGIN TRANSACTION;

UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE id = 2;

-- If both succeeded:
COMMIT;

-- If something went wrong:
-- ROLLBACK;
```

### SAVEPOINT Example

```sql
BEGIN TRANSACTION;

INSERT INTO orders (customer_id, total) VALUES (1, 250);
SAVEPOINT order_created;

INSERT INTO order_items (order_id, product_id, quantity) VALUES (1, 5, 2);

-- If order_items fails, roll back to savepoint but keep the order
ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT order_created;
```

### Transaction Isolation Levels

| Level | Dirty Read | Non-repeatable Read | Phantom Read |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| READ UNCOMMITTED | Possible | Possible | Possible |
| READ COMMITTED | Prevented | Possible | Possible |
| REPEATABLE READ | Prevented | Prevented | Possible |
| SERIALIZABLE | Prevented | Prevented | Prevented |

> **Key Point:** Transactions ensure that related operations succeed or fail together. Always commit or rollback to release locks and resources.

quiz.question: Which statement saves all changes in a transaction?
